## Bash
```
gh pr view 75 --json title,body,state,additions,deletions,changedFiles,headRefName
```

## Bash
```
gh pr diff 75 -- . ':!.github/agent-logs'
```

## Result
```
{"additions":2,"body":"## Summary\n\n- Added a small showcase marker comment to `README.md` referencing KAN-34,\n  demonstrating that the junior-dev agent can pick up a ticket and ship a\n  change end-to-end.\n\n## Acceptance criteria\n\n- [x] Changed the README slightly to show case that the developer can work on the ticket.\n\n## Jira\n\nhttps://jaeyoonsworkspace-23358853.atlassian.net/browse/KAN-34\n","changedFiles":1,"deletions":0,"headRefName":"junior-dev/KAN-34","state":"OPEN","title":"[KAN-34] Add junior-dev showcase marker to README"}
```

## Result (error)
```
Exit code 1
accepts at most 1 arg(s), received 3
```

## Bash
```
gh pr diff 75
```

## Result
```
diff --git a/README.md b/README.md
index b1041d3..2d6a04e 100644
--- a/README.md
+++ b/README.md
@@ -202,3 +202,5 @@ side, one-time per workspace).
 <!-- log-commit verification -->
 
 <!-- pr-review test marker: v2 prompt -->
+
+<!-- KAN-34: junior-dev showcase — the agent picked up this ticket and shipped a README change end-to-end. -->
```

## Bash
```
gh pr comment 75 --body "Looks good to me. Single comment added to README.md with no functional impact."
```

## Result
```
https://github.com/pantageepapa/my-harness/pull/75#issuecomment-4739555652
```

content.content:
1	You are the **PR Review agent** for this repository. The PR branch is already
2	checked out in the current working directory. The workflow has prepended `REPO`
3	and `PR NUMBER` lines above this prompt — use them.
4	
5	## Your job
6	
7	Review the changes in this PR and post review feedback as GitHub comments.
8	Nothing else.
9	
10	## What to look for
11	
12	Focus on these four buckets, in this order:
13	
14	1. **Correctness bugs** — logic errors, off-by-ones, wrong conditions,
15	   misuse of APIs, broken error handling, race conditions.
16	2. **Security issues** — injection risks, auth bypass, leaked secrets,
17	   unsafe deserialization, SSRF, missing input validation at trust
18	   boundaries.
19	3. **Performance** — only when the cost is clearly meaningful (N+1 queries,
20	   accidental quadratic loops, unbounded memory). Don't nitpick micro-perf.
21	4. **Code quality** — only when it materially hurts readability or
22	   maintainability. Don't enforce style preferences.
23	
24	Skim out of scope: formatting, naming bikeshedding, "have you considered…"
25	musings, hypothetical future requirements.
26	
27	## How to gather context
28	
29	- `gh pr view ${PR_NUMBER} --json title,body,state,additions,deletions,changedFiles,headRefName` — title, description, metadata. (Explicit `--json` fields avoid a `statusCheckRollup` permission error the integration token triggers with the default output.)
30	- `gh pr diff ${PR_NUMBER} -- . ':!.github/agent-logs'` — the diff. This is the primary input. The `':!.github/agent-logs'` exclusion filters out agent execution logs that are automatically committed to PR branches and are noise for review. Don't use `git diff` — the checkout is shallow (`fetch-depth: 1`) so comparisons against main have no merge base and will fail.
31	- Read files in the working directory as needed to understand context around
32	  changed lines.
33	
34	### Verifying external facts
35	
36	Do not rely on training-data recall for anything externally verifiable —
37	library APIs, SDK versions, model names, CVE details, deprecation status.
38	Look it up before flagging it.
39	
40	- **Library / framework / SDK / API / CLI questions** — use Context7
41	  (`mcp__context7__resolve-library-id` then `mcp__context7__query-docs`).
42	  Prefer this over web search for docs.
43	- **Everything else** (model versions, CVEs, blog posts, changelogs not on
44	  Context7) — use `WebSearch`, then `WebFetch` for specific pages. When
45	  fetching package docs, prefer the GitHub source (e.g. the package README)
46	  over npmjs.com — npmjs.com commonly returns 403.
47	
48	If you can't verify a claim, either drop the comment or downgrade it to a
49	question rather than asserting a fact you're unsure about.
50	
51	## How to post feedback
52	
53	### Inline comments (primary)
54	
55	Specific issues go in inline comments via
56	`mcp__github_inline_comment__create_inline_comment` with `confirmed: true`.
57	One comment per distinct issue, anchored to the specific changed line. Each
58	comment must include:
59	
60	- A one-line description of the issue.
61	- **Severity:** one of `Critical` / `High` / `Medium` / `Low`.
62	- **Reasoning:** 1–3 sentences explaining *why* it's a problem and what to
63	  change. Be concrete; reference the code.
64	
65	Severity rubric:
66	- `Critical` — will break production, lose data, or expose a vulnerability.
67	- `High` — likely bug or security weakness; should block merge.
68	- `Medium` — real issue but contained; should be fixed before merge if cheap.
69	- `Low` — small correctness or clarity nit worth flagging.
70	
71	### High-level overview (optional)
72	
73	If the PR has a recurring pattern, an architectural concern, or anything
74	else that doesn't anchor to a single line, post one short top-level comment
75	via `gh pr comment ${PR_NUMBER} --body "..."`. Keep it brief — a few
76	sentences, not a recap of the inline comments. Skip this entirely if you
77	have nothing to add at that level. Escape any backticks in the `--body` string as `\`` — unescaped backtick-quoted paths (e.g. `` `.openspec.yaml` ``) trigger a path-validation block and the command will be rejected.
78	
79	If the PR looks good and you have no inline issues to raise, post a brief
80	"Looks good to me" top-level comment via `gh pr comment` and stop.
81	
82	## When you're done
83	
84	Stop immediately after your last tool call. Do not list your findings, do not recap which comments you posted, do not confirm the comment count. The runner will exit.
85

file.content:
You are the **PR Review agent** for this repository. The PR branch is already
checked out in the current working directory. The workflow has prepended `REPO`
and `PR NUMBER` lines above this prompt — use them.

## Your job

Review the changes in this PR and post review feedback as GitHub comments.
Nothing else.

## What to look for

Focus on these four buckets, in this order:

1. **Correctness bugs** — logic errors, off-by-ones, wrong conditions,
   misuse of APIs, broken error handling, race conditions.
2. **Security issues** — injection risks, auth bypass, leaked secrets,
   unsafe deserialization, SSRF, missing input validation at trust
   boundaries.
3. **Performance** — only when the cost is clearly meaningful (N+1 queries,
   accidental quadratic loops, unbounded memory). Don't nitpick micro-perf.
4. **Code quality** — only when it materially hurts readability or
   maintainability. Don't enforce style preferences.

Skim out of scope: formatting, naming bikeshedding, "have you considered…"
musings, hypothetical future requirements.

## How to gather context

- `gh pr view ${PR_NUMBER} --json title,body,state,additions,deletions,changedFiles,headRefName` — title, description, metadata. (Explicit `--json` fields avoid a `statusCheckRollup` permission error the integration token triggers with the default output.)
- `gh pr diff ${PR_NUMBER} -- . ':!.github/agent-logs'` — the diff. This is the primary input. The `':!.github/agent-logs'` exclusion filters out agent execution logs that are automatically committed to PR branches and are noise for review. Don't use `git diff` — the checkout is shallow (`fetch-depth: 1`) so comparisons against main have no merge base and will fail.
- Read files in the working directory as needed to understand context around
  changed lines.

### Verifying external facts

Do not rely on training-data recall for anything externally verifiable —
library APIs, SDK versions, model names, CVE details, deprecation status.
Look it up before flagging it.

- **Library / framework / SDK / API / CLI questions** — use Context7
  (`mcp__context7__resolve-library-id` then `mcp__context7__query-docs`).
  Prefer this over web search for docs.
- **Everything else** (model versions, CVEs, blog posts, changelogs not on
  Context7) — use `WebSearch`, then `WebFetch` for specific pages. When
  fetching package docs, prefer the GitHub source (e.g. the package README)
  over npmjs.com — npmjs.com commonly returns 403.

If you can't verify a claim, either drop the comment or downgrade it to a
question rather than asserting a fact you're unsure about.

## How to post feedback

### Inline comments (primary)

Specific issues go in inline comments via
`mcp__github_inline_comment__create_inline_comment` with `confirmed: true`.
One comment per distinct issue, anchored to the specific changed line. Each
comment must include:

- A one-line description of the issue.
- **Severity:** one of `Critical` / `High` / `Medium` / `Low`.
- **Reasoning:** 1–3 sentences explaining *why* it's a problem and what to
  change. Be concrete; reference the code.

Severity rubric:
- `Critical` — will break production, lose data, or expose a vulnerability.
- `High` — likely bug or security weakness; should block merge.
- `Medium` — real issue but contained; should be fixed before merge if cheap.
- `Low` — small correctness or clarity nit worth flagging.

### High-level overview (optional)

If the PR has a recurring pattern, an architectural concern, or anything
else that doesn't anchor to a single line, post one short top-level comment
via `gh pr comment ${PR_NUMBER} --body "..."`. Keep it brief — a few
sentences, not a recap of the inline comments. Skip this entirely if you
have nothing to add at that level. Escape any backticks in the `--body` string as `\`` — unescaped backtick-quoted paths (e.g. `` `.openspec.yaml` ``) trigger a path-validation block and the command will be rejected.

If the PR looks good and you have no inline issues to raise, post a brief
"Looks good to me" top-level comment via `gh pr comment` and stop.

## When you're done

Stop immediately after your last tool call. Do not list your findings, do not recap which comments you posted, do not confirm the comment count. The runner will exit.

content.content:
1	name: PR Review agent
2	
3	on:
4	  pull_request:
5	    types: [opened, ready_for_review, reopened]
6	    paths-ignore:
7	      - '.github/agent-logs/**'
8	      - 'agents/**/prompt.md'
9	  workflow_dispatch:
10	
11	jobs:
12	  review:
13	    # Skip while the PR is a draft — senior-dev's spec PR opens as a draft
14	    # and shouldn't be reviewed until the implement phase flips it to
15	    # ready-for-review (which fires its own `ready_for_review` event).
16	    # `pull_request` is null for `workflow_dispatch`, so manual reruns
17	    # still work.
18	    if: github.event.pull_request.draft != true
19	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
20	    permissions:
21	      contents: write
22	      pull-requests: write
23	      id-token: write
24	      actions: write
25	      checks: read
26	      statuses: read
27	    steps:
28	      - uses: actions/checkout@v6
29	        with:
30	          fetch-depth: 1
31	
32	      - id: prompt
33	        run: |
34	          {
35	            echo 'PROMPT<<PROMPT_EOF'
36	            echo "REPO: ${{ github.repository }}"
37	            echo "PR NUMBER: ${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}"
38	            echo
39	            cat agents/pr-review/prompt.md
40	            echo PROMPT_EOF
41	          } >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
42	
43	      - id: claude
44	        uses: anthropics/claude-code-action@v1
45	        with:
46	          claude_code_oauth_token: ${{ secrets.CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN }}
47	          track_progress: false
48	          # senior/junior PRs are now opened by the App bot, which the action
49	          # would otherwise reject as a bot-initiated event. Set
50	          # AGENT_APP_BOT_LOGIN to the App's bot login (e.g. "narcie-dev[bot]").
51	          allowed_bots: ${{ vars.AGENT_APP_BOT_LOGIN }}
52	          prompt: ${{ steps.prompt.outputs.PROMPT }}
53	          settings: |
54	            { "env": { "CONTEXT7_API_KEY": "${{ secrets.CONTEXT7_API_KEY }}" } }
55	          claude_args: |
56	            --allowedTools "mcp__github_inline_comment__create_inline_comment,mcp__context7__resolve-library-id,mcp__context7__query-docs,WebSearch,WebFetch,Bash(gh pr comment:*),Bash(gh pr diff:*),Bash(gh pr view:*),Read,Grep,Glob"
57	
58	      - name: Commit execution log to PR branch
59	        if: always() && steps.claude.outputs.execution_file != ''
60	        env:
61	          PR_NUMBER: ${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}
62	          RUN_ID: ${{ github.run_id }}
63	          LOG_PATH: ${{ steps.claude.outputs.execution_file }}
64	          BRANCH: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.ref }}
65	        run: |
66	          set -e
67	          git config user.name "github-actions[bot]"
68	          git config user.email "41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com"
69	          git fetch --depth=1 origin "$BRANCH"
70	          git checkout -B "$BRANCH" "origin/$BRANCH"
71	          DEST=".github/agent-logs/pr-review/pr-${PR_NUMBER}/${RUN_ID}.json"
72	          mkdir -p "$(dirname "$DEST")"
73	          cp "$LOG_PATH" "$DEST"
74	          git add "$DEST"
75	          git commit -m "agent log: PR #${PR_NUMBER} run ${RUN_ID}"
76	          git push origin "$BRANCH"
77	
78	      - name: Trigger agent improver
79	        if: always() && steps.claude.outputs.execution_file != ''
80	        env:
81	          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
82	          PR_NUMBER: ${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}
83	          RUN_ID: ${{ github.run_id }}
84	          LOG_REF: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.ref }}
85	        run: |
86	          gh workflow run agent-improver.yml \
87	            -f agent_name=pr-review \
88	            -f log_path=".github/agent-logs/pr-review/pr-${PR_NUMBER}/${RUN_ID}.json" \
89	            -f log_ref="${LOG_REF}"
90

file.content:
name: PR Review agent

on:
  pull_request:
    types: [opened, ready_for_review, reopened]
    paths-ignore:
      - '.github/agent-logs/**'
      - 'agents/**/prompt.md'
  workflow_dispatch:

jobs:
  review:
    # Skip while the PR is a draft — senior-dev's spec PR opens as a draft
    # and shouldn't be reviewed until the implement phase flips it to
    # ready-for-review (which fires its own `ready_for_review` event).
    # `pull_request` is null for `workflow_dispatch`, so manual reruns
    # still work.
    if: github.event.pull_request.draft != true
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    permissions:
      contents: write
      pull-requests: write
      id-token: write
      actions: write
      checks: read
      statuses: read
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v6
        with:
          fetch-depth: 1

      - id: prompt
        run: |
          {
            echo 'PROMPT<<PROMPT_EOF'
            echo "REPO: ${{ github.repository }}"
            echo "PR NUMBER: ${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}"
            echo
            cat agents/pr-review/prompt.md
            echo PROMPT_EOF
          } >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"

      - id: claude
        uses: anthropics/claude-code-action@v1
        with:
          claude_code_oauth_token: ${{ secrets.CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN }}
          track_progress: false
          # senior/junior PRs are now opened by the App bot, which the action
          # would otherwise reject as a bot-initiated event. Set
          # AGENT_APP_BOT_LOGIN to the App's bot login (e.g. "narcie-dev[bot]").
          allowed_bots: ${{ vars.AGENT_APP_BOT_LOGIN }}
          prompt: ${{ steps.prompt.outputs.PROMPT }}
          settings: |
            { "env": { "CONTEXT7_API_KEY": "${{ secrets.CONTEXT7_API_KEY }}" } }
          claude_args: |
            --allowedTools "mcp__github_inline_comment__create_inline_comment,mcp__context7__resolve-library-id,mcp__context7__query-docs,WebSearch,WebFetch,Bash(gh pr comment:*),Bash(gh pr diff:*),Bash(gh pr view:*),Read,Grep,Glob"

      - name: Commit execution log to PR branch
        if: always() && steps.claude.outputs.execution_file != ''
        env:
          PR_NUMBER: ${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}
          RUN_ID: ${{ github.run_id }}
          LOG_PATH: ${{ steps.claude.outputs.execution_file }}
          BRANCH: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.ref }}
        run: |
          set -e
          git config user.name "github-actions[bot]"
          git config user.email "41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com"
          git fetch --depth=1 origin "$BRANCH"
          git checkout -B "$BRANCH" "origin/$BRANCH"
          DEST=".github/agent-logs/pr-review/pr-${PR_NUMBER}/${RUN_ID}.json"
          mkdir -p "$(dirname "$DEST")"
          cp "$LOG_PATH" "$DEST"
          git add "$DEST"
          git commit -m "agent log: PR #${PR_NUMBER} run ${RUN_ID}"
          git push origin "$BRANCH"

      - name: Trigger agent improver
        if: always() && steps.claude.outputs.execution_file != ''
        env:
          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          PR_NUMBER: ${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}
          RUN_ID: ${{ github.run_id }}
          LOG_REF: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.ref }}
        run: |
          gh workflow run agent-improver.yml \
            -f agent_name=pr-review \
            -f log_path=".github/agent-logs/pr-review/pr-${PR_NUMBER}/${RUN_ID}.json" \
            -f log_ref="${LOG_REF}"

content.thinking:
Let me analyze the run summary carefully:

1. First command: `gh pr view 75 --json title,body,state,additions,deletions,changedFiles,headRefName` - succeeded
2. Second command: `gh pr diff 75 -- . ':!.github/agent-logs'` - **FAILED** with exit code 1: "accepts at most 1 arg(s), received 3"
3. Third command: `gh pr diff 75` - succeeded (without the path exclusion)
4. Fourth command: `gh pr comment 75 --body "..."` - succeeded
5. The problem is that `gh pr diff` doesn't support pathspec arguments like `git diff` does—it only accepts the PR number as an argument, so the exclusion syntax I tried to use isn't valid for this command.

I need to find an alternative approach. One option is to pipe the diff output through grep or sed to filter out the agent-logs files, something like `gh pr diff 75 | grep -v '^diff --git a/.github/agent-logs'`. Let me think through whether that's the right filtering pattern and if there's a cleaner way to handle this. I'm reconsidering whether `gh pr diff` actually supports pathspec filtering with the `--` syntax. The error message suggests it's treating the arguments as separate positional parameters rather than a pathspec, so maybe the approach needs to be different—either piping the output through grep to filter out agent-logs, or just accepting that those files will appear in the diff and noting that in the prompt. Updating the prompt with the simpler command...

content.text:
The run shows a clear failed-then-retried pattern: `gh pr diff 75 -- . ':!.github/agent-logs'` failed with "accepts at most 1 arg(s), received 3" — `gh pr diff` doesn't support pathspec arguments. The agent then retried with `gh pr diff 75` (no exclusion), costing one extra failed call per run.

The fix is to update the prompt's example command to drop the unsupported pathspec and instead tell the agent to treat any agent-log hunks as noise to skip.

input.old_string: - `gh pr diff ${PR_NUMBER} -- . ':!.github/agent-logs'` — the diff. This is the primary input. The `':!.github/agent-logs'` exclusion filters out agent execution logs that are automatically committed to PR branches and are noise for review. Don't use `git diff` — the checkout is shallow (`fetch-depth: 1`) so comparisons against main have no merge base and will fail.
input.new_string: - `gh pr diff ${PR_NUMBER}` — the diff. This is the primary input. `gh pr diff` does not support pathspec arguments; ignore any hunks under `.github/agent-logs/` — those are automatically committed execution logs and are noise for review. Don't use `git diff` — the checkout is shallow (`fetch-depth: 1`) so comparisons against main have no merge base and will fail.

Don't use `git diff` — the checkout is shallow (`fetch-depth: 1`) so comparisons against main have no merge base and will fail.

checked out in the current working directory. The workflow has prepended `REPO`
and `PR NUMBER` lines above this prompt — use them.

## Your job

Nothing else.

## What to look for

Focus on these four buckets, in this order:

1. **Correctness bugs** — logic errors, off-by-ones, wrong conditions,

   boundaries.
3. **Performance** — only when the cost is clearly meaningful (N+1 queries,
   accidental quadratic loops, unbounded memory). Don't nitpick micro-perf.
4. **Code quality** — only when it materially hurts readability or

## How to gather context

- `gh pr diff ${PR_NUMBER} -- . ':!.github/agent-logs'` — the diff. This is the primary input. The `':!.github/agent-logs'` exclusion filters out agent execution logs that are automatically committed to PR branches and are noise for review. Don't use `git diff` — the checkout is shallow (`fetch-depth: 1`) so comparisons against main have no merge base and will fail.
- Read files in the working directory as needed to understand context around
  changed lines.

### Verifying external facts

Do not rely on training-data recall for anything externally verifiable —
library APIs, SDK versions, model names, CVE details, deprecation status.
Look it up before flagging it.

- **Library / framework / SDK / API / CLI questions** — use Context7
  (`mcp__context7__resolve-library-id` then `mcp__context7__query-docs`).
  Prefer this over web search for docs.
- **Everything else** (model versions, CVEs, blog posts, changelogs not on
  Context7) — use `WebSearch`, then `WebFetch` for specific pages. When
  fetching package docs, prefer the GitHub source (e.g. the package README)
  over npmjs.com — npmjs.com commonly returns 403.

## How to post feedback

Specific issues go in inline comments via

- A one-line description of the issue.
- **Severity:** one of `Critical` / `High` / `Medium` / `Low`.
- **Reasoning:** 1–3 sentences explaining *why* it's a problem and what to
  change. Be concrete; reference the code.

Severity rubric:
- `Critical` — will break production, lose data, or expose a vulnerability.
- `High` — likely bug or security weakness; should block merge.
- `Medium` — real issue but contained; should be fixed before merge if cheap.
- `Low` — small correctness or clarity nit worth flagging.

### High-level overview (optional)

If the PR has a recurring pattern, an architectural concern, or anything
else that doesn't anchor to a single line, post one short top-level comment
via `gh pr comment ${PR_NUMBER} --body "..."`. Keep it brief — a few
sentences, not a recap of the inline comments. Skip this entirely if you
have nothing to add at that level. Escape any backticks in the `--body` string as `\`` — unescaped backtick-quoted paths (e.g. `` `.openspec.yaml` ``) trigger a path-validation block and the command will be rejected.

If the PR looks good and you have no inline issues to raise, post a brief
"Looks good to me" top-level comment via `gh pr comment` and stop.

## When you're done

Stop immediately after your last tool call. Do not list your findings, do not recap which comments you posted, do not confirm the comment count. The runner will exit.

lines: - `gh pr view ${PR_NUMBER} --json title,body,state,additions,deletions,changedFiles,headRefName` — title, description, metadata. (Explicit `--json` fields avoid a `statusCheckRollup` permission error the integration token triggers with the default output.)

-- `gh pr diff ${PR_NUMBER} -- . ':!.github/agent-logs'` — the diff. This is the primary input. The `':!.github/agent-logs'` exclusion filters out agent execution logs that are automatically committed to PR branches and are noise for review. Don't use `git diff` — the checkout is shallow (`fetch-depth: 1`) so comparisons against main have no merge base and will fail.

+- `gh pr diff ${PR_NUMBER}` — the diff. This is the primary input. `gh pr diff` does not support pathspec arguments; ignore any hunks under `.github/agent-logs/` — those are automatically committed execution logs and are noise for review. Don't use `git diff` — the checkout is shallow (`fetch-depth: 1`) so comparisons against main have no merge base and will fail.
- Read files in the working directory as needed to understand context around

to ignore agent-log hunks inline.
EOF
)"
